text.title: Substack-to-Product Funnel Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Substack-to-Product Funnel application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Paid tier MUST deliver a concrete benefit (gated post, monthly call, archive, etc.); 'support the publication' tip-jar framing is rejected.
rule.rationale: Tip-jar paid tiers stagnate; benefit-based tiers compound and feed hard-product conversions.

rule.statement: ≥3 recommendation partners MUST be agreed AND wired into the on-publish flow; recommendations-off launches are rejected.
rule.rationale: Recommendations are Substack's compounding loop; off = leaves discovery on the table.

rule.statement: ≥1 cross-post per month MUST ship with a peer publication; zero-cross-post months are rejected.
rule.rationale: Cross-posts move subs at 5-10x the rate of normal posts.

rule.statement: Welcome sequence email 4 MUST carry the hard-product CTA; deferring to email 8+ is rejected.
rule.rationale: Email 4 hits the highest open rate window before churn; deferring loses the conversion.

rule.statement: KPI set MUST track hard_product_conversions, not just paid_subs.
rule.rationale: Paid subs without hard-product conversions is a soft-product success masking a hard-product failure.
